overview: A single Go backend service exposes the existing Todo REST API plus a new POST /todos/bulk-delete endpoint that accepts up to 100 UUIDs, enforces per-item ownership, and returns a categorized result summary (deleted / notFound / forbidden). The service is stateless over an in-memory store, authenticated via bearer tokens, and rate-limited, with no new components required to fulfill the spec.
requirements: Reject empty, oversized, malformed, or non-UUID inputs with 400 and a structured error
Require bearer authentication; enforce same ownership rules as single DELETE
Skip non-existent IDs without failing the batch; classify as notFound
Classify IDs owned by other users as forbidden and do not delete them
Perform deletions atomically; roll back on partial storage failure
Operation is idempotent across repeated requests
Apply rate limiting consistent with other write endpoints
Log each bulk delete with requester identity and affected IDs
Preserve existing endpoint behavior and error-format conventions

components.name: todo-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Todo API
  version: 1.1.0
  description: Todo management API with bulk delete support.
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
  /todos:
    get:
      summary: List todos for authenticated user
      security: [{BearerAuth: []}]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo'}
    post:
      summary: Create a todo
      security: [{BearerAuth: []}]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/TodoInput'}
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo'}
  /todos/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get a todo
      security: [{BearerAuth: []}]
      parameters:
        - {name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string, format: uuid}}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK, content: {application/json: {schema: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo'}}}}
        '404': {description: Not found}
    delete:
      summary: Delete a todo
      security: [{BearerAuth: []}]
      parameters:
        - {name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string, format: uuid}}
      responses:
        '204': {description: Deleted}
        '403': {description: Forbidden}
        '404': {description: Not found}
  /todos/bulk-delete:
    post:
      summary: Bulk delete todos
      description: Deletes up to 100 todos in one request. De-duplicates IDs silently. Returns per-item result.
      security: [{BearerAuth: []}]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/BulkDeleteRequest'}
      responses:
        '200':
          description: At least one deletion succeeded or all were no-ops on valid input
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/BulkDeleteResponse'}
        '400': {description: Invalid request, content: {application/json: {schema: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/Error'}}}}
        '401': {description: Unauthenticated}
        '429': {description: Rate limited}
components:
  securitySchemes:
    BearerAuth: {type: http, scheme: bearer}
  schemas:
    Todo:
      type: object
      properties:
        id: {type: string, format: uuid}
        ownerId: {type: string}
        title: {type: string}
        completed: {type: boolean}
        createdAt: {type: string, format: date-time}
    TodoInput:
      type: object
      required: [title]
      properties:
        title: {type: string}
        completed: {type: boolean}
    BulkDeleteRequest:
      type: object
      required: [ids]
      properties:
        ids:
          type: array
          minItems: 1
          maxItems: 100
          items: {type: string, format: uuid}
    BulkDeleteResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        deletedCount: {type: integer}
        deleted: {type: array, items: {type: string, format: uuid}}
        notFound: {type: array, items: {type: string, format: uuid}}
        forbidden: {type: array, items: {type: string, format: uuid}}
    Error:
      type: object
      properties:
        code: {type: string}
        message: {type: string}
        details: {type: object, additionalProperties: true}
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Implement a Go service using net/http on port 9090. Provide a Todo management API with in-memory thread-safe storage (sync.RWMutex, map[uuid]Todo). Authentication: require 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header; treat token as the userId (simple stub). Return 401 if missing.

Endpoints:
- GET /health -> 200 {"status":"ok"}
- GET /todos -> list caller-owned todos
- POST /todos -> create todo owned by caller (generate UUIDv4 id)
- GET /todos/{id} -> 200 if owned, 403 if owned by another user, 404 if missing
- DELETE /todos/{id} -> 204 if owned & deleted, 403 if other owner, 404 if missing
- POST /todos/bulk-delete -> bulk delete

Bulk delete behavior:
- Parse JSON body {ids: [uuid,...]}.
- 400 if body malformed, ids missing, empty, or > 100 items, or any id is not a valid UUID. Error format {code, message, details}.
- Silently de-duplicate ids (document in OpenAPI).
- For each unique id: classify as deleted | notFound | forbidden (owned by other user).
- Perform deletions under a single mutex lock to ensure atomic consistency; only actually delete entries after all authorization checks succeed for those ids (skip others). If any storage error, roll back already-deleted entries in this batch (keep pre-images).
- Idempotent: re-deleting already-deleted ids returns them in notFound, not errors.
- Respond 200 with {deletedCount, deleted, notFound, forbidden}.
- Log each bulk delete: requester userId + sorted id list + outcome counts.

Cross-cutting:
- Apply a simple in-memory token-bucket rate limiter per userId on write endpoints (POST/DELETE/bulk-delete), e.g., 60 req/min; respond 429 when exceeded.
- CORS: allow all origins, methods GET,POST,DELETE,OPTIONS, headers Authorization,Content-Type.
- Consistent JSON error format {code, message, details?} for all 4xx/5xx.
- Structured logs to stdout.

Dockerfile: multi-stage Go build, distroless/static final image, EXPOSE 9090, CMD runs the binary.
